title: Drowning - Recognition and Immediate Action
immediate_steps: Ensure your own safety first before attempting a rescue
Immediately remove them from water if it is safe to do so
Check breathing and responsiveness as soon as the person is out of water
Begin CPR if the person is not breathing normally

title: Drowning - Rescue Techniques and Safety
immediate_steps: Prioritize your own safety to avoid becoming a victim
Use reaching or throwing methods such as sticks, ropes, or flotation devices instead of entering the water when possible
If entering water is necessary, approach cautiously from behind to avoid being grabbed
Support the person's head above water and move them to a safe area
avoid: Avoid rough handling, as the person may be panicking and unintentionally pulling the rescuer underwater

title: Drowning - CPR After Rescue
immediate_steps: Start CPR without delay. Begin with chest compressions followed by rescue breaths if trained
Clear visible water or debris from the mouth if present
Continue cycles of compressions and breaths until the person begins breathing or emergency medical help arrives
avoid: Do not delay CPR to attempt to remove water from the lungs

title: Drowning - Recovery and Post Rescue Care
immediate_steps: Monitor closely and ensure immediate medical attention
Place the person in a recovery position on their side to keep the airway open and prevent choking
Keep them warm using blankets or clothing to prevent hypothermia
Watch for delayed complications such as breathing difficulty, coughing, or chest pain
avoid: Do not assume full recovery without hospital evaluation, as internal water inhalation may cause secondary complications
